page.title: Type 2 Diabetes — Disease Intelligence | OSMF
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus: 127 alterations and 767 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: 1.6-1.7% at 7 years on standard care (population-level cohorts UK and US)
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: 46-58% at 5 years post-bariatric surgery; up to 86% at 1 year with >15% total weight loss via intensive dietary intervention (DiRECT trial: 36% at 2 years)
remission.barrier_detail: Remission is biologically achievable and demonstrated in multiple large RCTs but almost never offered as the primary clinical strategy; default is indefinite pharmacotherapy escalation
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — ADA Standards of Care added formal remission definition (HbA1c <6.5% off all glucose-lowering meds 3+ months) and early GLP-1/SGLT2 use; prior first-line was metformin-only since ~2006
remission.outdated_treatments: Sulfonylureas (hypoglycemia risk, weight gain, no CV benefit) remain widely prescribed despite GLP-1 and SGLT2 inhibitors showing CV and renal protection; insulin as early add-on before weight-loss intervention is attempted
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Berberine (equivalent HbA1c lowering to metformin in some trials), time-restricted eating, very low carbohydrate diet, resistance training as primary intervention (not adjunct)
remission.frontier_signal: Tirzepatide achieving 15-22% body weight loss with high remission rates; semaglutide 2.4mg SUSTAIN trials; islet cell encapsulation (Vertex VX-880, Phase 1/2)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
